title: trifolium pratense subsp. nivale
intro: Trifolium pratense subsp. nivale, also known as white clover, is a subspecies of perennial legume that grows in white or pink flowers and has a distinctive white or pink coloration. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues, due to its high content of isoflavones and other compounds. The health benefits of white clover include improved gut health, reduced symptoms of menopause, and enhanced antioxidant activity. White clover can be prepared as a tea, capsule, or dried extract, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote relaxation and support overall well-being.
